title: 10 best herbal tinctures for nerve pain
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for nerve pain are liquid extracts made from plants that help alleviate discomfort and inflammation in the nervous system. They work by reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and improving blood flow to the affected areas. The best herbal tinctures for nerve pain include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Hypericum perforatum, Avena sativa, Zingiber officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, Ginkgo biloba, Cannabis sativa, Corydalis yanhusuo, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, nerve pain can severely impact daily life, leading to insomnia, depression, and anxiety, ultimately affecting relationships, work performance, and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. This can help alleviate symptoms of nerve damage, such as numbness, tingling, and shooting pain. Additionally, licorice's flavonoids and saponins may also help to calm irritated nerves, promoting a sense of relaxation and ease in individuals suffering from neuropathic pain.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with nerve damage or neuropathy.

Flavonoids: Licorice contains a range of flavonoids, including liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, that have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect against oxidative stress and nerve damage.
Saponins: The saponin content in licorice tincture has been found to have a neuroprotective effect, helping to preserve the integrity of nerve tissue and reduce pain signals transmitted to the brain.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps alleviate nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting the release of anti-inflammatory cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and pain associated with nerve damage.

Licopyranocoumarin: This phenolic compound has shown analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce nerve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing inflammation in the nervous system.

Isoflavane: This isoflavane glycoside has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to alleviate nerve pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, promoting tissue repair and regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The primary part used in tinctures for nerve pain due to its high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Used in tinctures for nerve pain as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help in reducing inflammation and pain.
Barks: Employed in tinctures for nerve pain as it contains flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 20 kg of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the fall season when the plant is mature.
Chop the roots into smaller pieces and soak them in 70% ethanol at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks at room temperature, shaking the container every day for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass containers and store it in a cool, dry place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique blend of compounds, including capsaicin and antioxidants, that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and alleviate neuralgia. The capsaicin in bell peppers binds to pain receptors, desensitizing them and interrupting the pain signals sent to the brain. Meanwhile, the antioxidants combat oxidative stress and tissue damage, further reducing nerve inflammation and promoting a more rapid recovery from episodes of nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Capsaicin (found in chili peppers): This natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound has been shown to reduce nerve pain by blocking the production of substance P, a chemical that transmits pain signals to the brain.
Ginger: Ginger's active compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with nerve pain.

Turmeric/Curcumin: Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and promoting neural health.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin is a potent analgesic that binds to pain receptors in the skin, interrupting the transmission of pain signals to the brain and providing relief from nerve pain.

Volatile oil components (such as beta-caryophyllene, beta-pinene, and limonene): These terpenic compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.

Flavonoids (such as kaempferol and quercetin): These phenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system, thereby alleviating nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Fruits (commonly known as peppers) are used to make tinctures for nerve pain due to their high capsaicin content, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves are used to make tinctures for nerve pain due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
Seeds: Seeds are used to make tinctures for nerve pain due to their high capsaicin content and potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of ripe capsicum annuum fruits at the peak of their flavor and color usually in late summer.
Chop the capsicum annuum fruits into small pieces to increase their surface area for better solvent penetration.
Combine 250g of chopped capsicum annuum fruits with 750ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2-3 weeks shaking the container daily to facilitate solvent extraction.
Filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain arnica tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its ability to penetrate deep into tissues, targeting areas affected by inflammation and damage. The natural anti-inflammatory properties in mountain arnica work to reduce swelling and ease pressure on nerve endings, providing relief from shooting pains, numbness, and tingling sensations. Additionally, the tincture's analgesic properties help to block pain signals, offering long-lasting comfort and relaxation for those suffering from chronic nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain caused by nerve compression, pinched nerves, or inflammation in the nerves, which can alleviate nerve pain.

Analgesic: The analgesic properties of arnica tincture help to numb the affected area, providing relief from acute and chronic nerve pain, including conditions like sciatica, shingles, and fibromyalgia.
Antioxidant: Arnica's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the nerves, which can contribute to nerve pain and degeneration.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin helps with nerve pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can contribute to nerve pain relief.
Liquiritigenin: Liquiritigenin helps with nerve pain by acting as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with nerve damage.
Helenalin: Helenalin helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which can contribute to nerve damage and pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpene lactones than the flowers, making them a popular choice for tinctures that require more potent pain relief.
Leaves: Although less commonly used than flowers and roots, the leaves are still used in some tinctures due to their ability to provide relief from nerve pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers and leaves in late summer and early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area using a dehydrator at 95°f for 12 hours.
Grind the dried arnica montana flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 1 part of the arnica montana powder with 2 parts of a neutral solvent such as vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine. This increase in neurotransmitter availability can help to reduce the firing of abnormal nerve signals, leading to relief from nerve pain symptoms. Additionally, St John's Wort tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which can further contribute to its analgesic effects by reducing inflammation that may be contributing to nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Helps to stabilize and protect the nerves by reducing inflammation, promoting blood flow, and modulating neurotransmitters, which can help alleviate nerve pain.
Hypericin: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing pain perception and inflammation associated with nerve damage or injury.
Flavonoids: Act as antioxidants, scavenging free radicals that can contribute to nerve damage and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to nerve pain.

Naphthodianthrones (Hypericin and Pseudo-hypericin): Inhibit the reuptake of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which helps to alleviate nerve pain by promoting a sense of well-being and reducing anxiety.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Exhibit strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in nerves, thereby alleviating nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in hypericin and hyperforin, two compounds responsible for the plant's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems contain hypericin and hyperforin, which help to reduce nerve pain and inflammation.
Flowers: They are a good source of hypericin and hyperforin, making them effective in treating nerve pain and promoting relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers at the peak of flowering stage in summer.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 35-40 percent ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique compound called valerenic acid, which has been shown to interact with neurotransmitters in the brain to reduce inflammation and modulate pain signals. This can provide relief from conditions such as sciatica, shingles, and peripheral neuropathy. By reducing inflammation and soothing irritated nerves, valerian tinctures can help alleviate burning sensations, numbness, and tingling that are common symptoms of nerve damage.
remedy_properties: GABA-enhancing properties: Valerian root has been shown to increase the activity of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate pain and anxiety, which can contribute to nerve pain relief.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian tincture contains compounds like isovaleric acid, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with nerve damage or conditions like neuropathy.

Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian root has been found to exhibit muscle-relaxing properties, which can help alleviate spasms, cramping, and stiffness associated with nerve pain, particularly in cases of peripheral neuropathy.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic Acid: As a GABA receptor agonist, valerenic acid helps to calm the nervous system, reducing nerve pain and anxiety.
Valtrate (Isovaltrate): Acting as a GABA receptor agonist, valtrate helps to inhibit the excitatory neurotransmitter glutamate, thereby reducing nerve pain and promoting relaxation.
Valerenol: With its sedative and anxiolytic properties, valerenol helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate nerve pain, promoting a sense of calmness and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid and valerenol, which contribute to their analgesic and sedative properties.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its pain-relieving effects.
Leaves: Also contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh valeriana officinalis roots and 1 part of dried valeriana officinalis roots from a trusted source.
Chop 1 part of fresh valeriana officinalis roots into small pieces and combine with 1 part of dried valeriana officinalis roots.
Steep 1 part of chopped valeriana officinalis roots in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka for 2 weeks in a clean glass jar.
Strain the liquid from the jar using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container discard the solids.
Store the resulting herbal tincture in a cool dark place and allow it to mature for 4 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains compounds that have a potent ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying factors of nerve pain. The herb's active ingredients also show promise in modulating neurotransmitters, such as GABA and serotonin, to alleviate neuropathic symptoms like numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Additionally, brahmi's antioxidant properties help protect against damage caused by free radicals, further contributing to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. This natural remedy offers a potential solution for individuals seeking relief from chronic nerve pain without resorting to pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to nerve damage and pain.

Neuroprotective effects: Brahmi's neuroprotective compounds, such as brahminic acid, may help preserve the integrity of nervous tissue and promote the regeneration of damaged neurons, reducing nerve pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nerves, which can contribute to pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Bacosides: Bacosides are a group of saponin glycosides that have been shown to have neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce nerve pain by protecting nerve cells from damage and reducing inflammation in the nervous system.

Bacosterol: Bacosterol is a triterpenoid saponin that has been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate nerve pain by reducing pain perception and inflammation in the nervous system.

Monnieroside: Monnieroside is a bacoside that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce nerve pain by protecting nerve cells from oxidative stress and reducing inflammation in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including bacosides, which are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Roots: The roots are another key part used for tinctures, as they contain a rich mixture of alkaloids and glycosides that contribute to the plant's cognitive and analgesic effects.
Stems: The stems are also used to make tinctures, although to a lesser extent, as they contain some of the same bioactive compounds as the leaves and roots, albeit in smaller quantities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried bacopa monnieri leaves and stems to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Chop 1 part of the harvested plant material to 2 parts of 80-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove solids.
Label and store the bacopa monnieri tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds that interact with the body's nervous system. The essential oils and flavonoids in angelica root have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in affected nerves. Additionally, angelica's antispasmodic effects can help relax muscle contractions and ease tension, providing relief from chronic nerve pain conditions such as fibromyalgia and sciatica.
remedy_properties: Beta-Pinene: This monoterpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce nerve inflammation and pain by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Angelolide: A sesquiterpene lactone, Angelolide exhibits potent anti-nociceptive (pain-relieving) activity, inhibiting the transmission of pain signals in the nervous system and reducing neuronal excitability.

Phenolic Acids: The phenolic acids present in Angelica tinctures, such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to nerve damage and pain.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine is an alkaloid that helps with nerve pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which reduce swelling and damage to nerve cells.

Ligustilide: Ligustilide is a sesquiterpene lactone that helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory substances and promoting the release of neurotransmitters that block pain signals.

Falcarinol: Falcarinol is a phenolic compound that helps with nerve pain by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which protect nerve cells from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and furanocoumarins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Barks: The bark of Angelica archangelica contains sesquiterpenes and other compounds that contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a popular choice for tinctures.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures due to their rich content of sesquiterpenes, which are believed to have pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and rhizomes in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Chop the dried angelica archangelica roots and rhizomes into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part plant material to 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks before straining and storing the tincture in a cool dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenes present in maypop work synergistically to reduce inflammation and relieve pressure on damaged nerves, providing fast and effective relief from chronic neuropathic pain. Additionally, maypop's ability to modulate the nervous system's response to pain signals helps to break the cycle of discomfort, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Passionflower alkaloids: Maypop tincture contains alkaloids like flavonoids and lignans that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, helping to calm inflammation and reduce nerve pain.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids present in maypop tincture have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate nerve irritation and swelling, thereby reducing pain and discomfort.
Fatty acid esters: Fatty acid esters found in maypop tincture have analgesic (pain-relieving) and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in treating nerve pain and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Isophorone: It has sedative and anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that can exacerbate nerve pain.

Rotundifuran (also known as rotundifuran sesquiterpene lactone): This compound has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain perception.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect nerves from damage and reduce pain and inflammation associated with nerve disorders.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, responsible for the herb's pain-relieving and sedative properties.
Roots: They are rich in flavonoids and glycosides, which contribute to the tincture's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Fruits: They are a source of passifloric acid, a compound that has been shown to have a pain-relieving effect and is often used in combination with other parts to create a more effective tincture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in large quantities to ensure adequate material.
Clean the harvested material thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one part of the cleaned passiflora material with two parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks to allow for extraction of the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains menthol, a natural analgesic that directly targets nerve endings to reduce inflammation and numbness. The cooling properties of peppermint also help to ease pressure on the nerves, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. Additionally, peppermint's antispasmodic effects can help to calm muscle spasms, which can contribute to nerve pain. Overall, herbal peppermint tinctures provide a natural and effective way to manage and alleviate nerve pain, promoting overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a primary compound found in peppermint oil, helps to numb and desensitize nerves, providing relief from nerve pain by blocking pain receptors.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling around nerve endings, which can contribute to chronic nerve pain conditions such as sciatica or neuropathy.

Gastric calming effects: Peppermint's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms can also help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), a condition commonly associated with nerve pain and discomfort in the abdomen and pelvis.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool, a terpene, acts as an analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent, which may help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain signaling.
Menthol: Menthol, a monoterpenoid, has a cooling effect on nerve endings, which may help numb the pain, providing temporary relief from nerve pain and inflammation.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with nerve pain, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Contain a similar concentration of essential oils as the leaves, including menthol and menthone, providing relief from nerve pain.
Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, including menthol and menthone, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and help alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a clean environment for 2 hours.
Combine the dried leaves and flowers with 2 cups of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain and filter the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's active compounds, including rosmarinic acid and camphor, work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, providing relief from chronic pain. Additionally, sage has a natural analgesic effect, helping to numb the painful sensation. As a result, herbal sage tinctures can effectively alleviate nerve pain associated with conditions such as neuropathy, fibromyalgia, and multiple sclerosis.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Sage's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm down overactive nerves, reducing muscle cramps and spasms associated with nerve pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected nerves, which can contribute to nerve pain relief.
Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help protect nerves from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic nerve pain conditions like neuropathy.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, thereby alleviating nerve pain.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm nerve pain.
Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid compound that has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which can help reduce nerve pain and promote healing in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: High in salvianolic acid and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate nerve pain.
Stems: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may contribute to their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects in nerve pain relief.
Buds: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested salvia officinalis in a warm dark place for 7-10 days at 35-40°c.
Combine 250g of dried salvia officinalis with 750ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with nerve pain because its unique combination of flavonoids, terpenoids, and volatile oils creates a powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effect. This synergy calms irritated nerves, reducing inflammation and swelling that can cause sharp, shooting pains. Additionally, chamomile's GABA-like properties help regulate the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, which are often comorbid with nerve pain. As a result, herbal chamomile tinctures provide a natural and effective way to soothe and alleviate chronic nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and irritation in the nervous system, which can contribute to nerve pain relief.
Anxiolytic: Chamomile's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common underlying causes of nerve pain, by calming the nervous system.
Antispasmodic: Chamomile tincture's antispasmodic property helps relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to nerve pain relief and overall comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce nerve inflammation and alleviate pain.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain azulene, a potent antioxidant that soothes and calms irritated nerves, reducing symptoms of nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning when they are fully open for maximum potency.
Rinse the harvested flowers with filtered water and pat them dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Combine 200g of the dried or fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can damage nerve tissue. This leads to a decrease in nerve pain associated with conditions such as sciatica, peripheral neuropathy, and fibromyalgia. Additionally, curcumin's ability to modulate the body's response to pain signals can provide natural relief from chronic nerve pain, making turmeric tinctures a popular natural remedy for those seeking alternative pain management options.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to nerve pain.
Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in turmeric, such as cineole and borneol, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to numb the pain and reduce swelling around damaged nerves.

Antioxidant Activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect against free radical damage, which can contribute to nerve damage and inflammation, thereby reducing the likelihood of chronic nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing nerve pain.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat various pains, including nerve pain, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried turmeric root, one part organic vodka and filterable water.
Combine the ingredients in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks.
Shake the mixture daily for 30 seconds to ensure even extraction of active compounds.
After the preparation time is complete strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Transfer the curcuma longa tincture to amber glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive compounds found in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to effectively reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the nervous system. By reducing the swelling and irritation that can occur in damaged nerves, lemongrass tinctures help to quiet down the nerve's overactive signals, providing relief from symptoms like tingling, numbness, and burning sensations. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe nerve pain and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemongrass contains anti-inflammatory compounds like citral and geraniol, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain by decreasing swelling and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of lemongrass tincture help protect nerves from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing the risk of nerve degeneration and pain.

Anxiolytic and sedative effects: Lemongrass tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety, stress, and tension that often exacerbate nerve pain, promoting relaxation and pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: Contain citral, a natural pain-reliever that helps reduce inflammation and numbness associated with nerve damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers at dawn for maximum potency.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and combine with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids after two hours.

Label and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place, using it as directed, at a dosage of 20-30 drops sublingually three times daily for optimal relief from nerve pain within four to six weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique compound called kavalactones, which have been shown to interact with the brain's neurotransmitters to produce a calming effect on the nervous system. This can help to reduce the sensitivity of nerve fibers, thereby alleviating the pain and discomfort associated with conditions such as neuropathy and fibromyalgia. Additionally, kava's ability to calm the mind and promote relaxation can also play a significant role in reducing overall anxiety levels, which can often exacerbate nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (Anti-Anxiety) Properties: Kava's anxiolytic effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are often underlying causes of nerve pain, allowing for a sense of calmness and relaxation.
Muscle Relaxant Properties: Kava's muscle-relaxing properties can help ease muscle cramps, spasms, and stiffness associated with nerve damage, promoting comfort and reducing discomfort.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Kava's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce inflammation in the body, which may contribute to nerve pain and discomfort, by reducing swelling and pain in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the kava plant are rich in kavalactones, which have been shown to have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for relieving nerve pain.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the kava plant contain high concentrations of kavalactones, which are believed to interact with neurotransmitters in the brain to reduce anxiety and pain perception, making them useful for treating nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh kava root or rhizome weighing 200-300 grams from a reputable source.
Clean and chop the harvested kava into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Combine the chopped kava with 1000ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Berberine has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of nerve pain such as numbness, tingling, and burning sensations. Additionally, barberry's ability to stabilize and repair damaged nerve cells may also contribute to its effectiveness in reducing nerve pain. By addressing the underlying causes of nerve damage, herbal barberry tinctures can provide natural relief from chronic nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Berberine: A bioactive compound found in barberry root bark, berberine has potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce nerve inflammation and alleviate pain associated with conditions such as sciatica, neuropathy, and fibromyalgia.

Vasodilation: The tannins and alkaloids present in barberry tincture help to dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow and oxygenation to damaged nerves, which can aid in the healing process and reduce pain caused by nerve compression or damage.

Antioxidant activity: Barberry's rich antioxidant profile helps protect nerves from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and degeneration of nerves, ultimately leading to conditions like peripheral neuropathy.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural compound that has been shown to have potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in alleviating nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: Contains a high concentration of alkaloids, including berberine, which has been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis vulgaris roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces weighing approximately 100g per batch.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days allowing for active compounds extraction.
Strain and filter the resulting liquid removing solids and store the tincture in airtight containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, work together to reduce inflammation and relieve pressure on damaged nerves. This allows for improved blood flow and reduced transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing welcome relief from nerve pain symptoms. Additionally, fennel's nervine properties help to soothe and calm the nervous system, further reducing discomfort and promoting a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Anethole: Fennel contains a significant amount of anethole, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce nerve inflammation and alleviate pain.

Flavonoids: Fennel's flavonoid content, including quercetin and kaempferol, has been shown to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate nerve pain.
Terpenes: The terpene content in fennel tinctures, such as limonene and pinene, has been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant are rich in anethole, a compound that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in relieving nerve pain.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a high amount of estragole, a natural oil that has been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including nerve pain due to its ability to reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems at the peak of their potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces weighing approximately 100 grams.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of vodka or 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its unique composition, which includes anti-inflammatory compounds and antioxidants. These bioactive compounds, such as sesamin and sesamolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often underlying causes of nerve pain. Additionally, sesame tinctures contain omega-6 fatty acids, which can help to regulate the body's response to inflammation and promote healthy nervous system function. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal sesame tinctures can provide effective relief from nerve pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-nociceptive compounds: Sesame tincture contains sesamin and sesamolin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in sesame tincture, such as sesamin and sesamol, help protect nerve cells from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic pain conditions.

Neuroprotective compounds: Sesame tincture contains neuroprotective compounds like sesamin and sesamolin, which have been shown to promote the growth of new neurons and prevent the degradation of existing neural tissue, potentially helping to alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The sesame root contains a high concentration of sesamin, which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing nerve pain.
Rhyzomes: Sesame rhyzomes contain sesamolin, which has been traditionally used to treat various types of pain, including neuralgia and sciatica.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sesamum indicum seeds from a trusted source, selecting those that are free of contaminants and impurities.
Clean and dry the harvested sesamum indicum seeds thoroughly, removing any debris or residual moisture.
Grind 100 grams of dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking care to avoid overheating.
Combine the ground sesamum indicum powder with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass container, allowing it to macerate for eight weeks, shaking daily.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles, labeling them with preparation date, dosage, and storage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its ability to soothe and calm irritated nerves. The isoflavones present in red clover have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which reduces swelling and inflammation around the affected area. This helps to alleviate numbness, tingling, and burning sensations often associated with nerve damage or neuropathy. Additionally, red clover's antioxidant properties help to protect nerves from further damage and promote healthy cellular function, leading to relief from chronic nerve pain.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Isoflavones in red clover tincture have been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which may help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in the tincture have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in nerves that contribute to pain.
Saponins: Saponins in red clover tincture may help modulate the activity of neurotransmitters involved in pain perception, leading to a reduction in nerve pain symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing nerve pain.
Flowers: Containing triterpenoids, such as rosmarinic acid, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality trifolium pratense flowers at peak bloom and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator or shade drying.
Combine one part dried trifolium pratense flowers with one part vodka in a clean glass jar, filling to the brim.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, adding a label with instructions for dosage of 20-30 drops three times daily as needed for nerve pain relief.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nerve pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are fibromyalgia, arthritis, and inflammation-related conditions. Herbal remedies like capsaicin, ginger, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate symptoms such as joint stiffness, muscle soreness, and numbness. Other herbs like passionflower, kava, and valerian root are used to treat anxiety and insomnia, which can be comorbid with nerve pain conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with nerve pain is a blend of Turmeric, Ashwagandha, and St. John's Wort. Turmeric contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory that reduces swelling and pain. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, helps to calm the nervous system and alleviate anxiety associated with nerve pain. St. John's Wort, a natural anti-depressant, also soothes nerve endings and promotes relaxation. This synergy of herbs works together to provide effective relief from nerve pain, promoting overall well-being and comfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains a wealth of bioactive compounds, including valerenic acid and valepotriates. These compounds have potent sedative and anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm nervous system irritation and reduce pain transmission. By modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and influencing the body's stress response, Valeriana officinalis tinctures can provide relief from nerve pain, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort. As a result, many people find it effective in managing conditions such as sciatica, multiple sclerosis, and neuropathic pain.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps with nerve pain by inhibiting the activity of neurotransmitters that contribute to anxiety and insomnia, thereby reducing nerve irritability.
Valeranone: Exhibits sedative and anxiolytic properties, which help alleviate nerve pain by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
Isovaleric acid: Demonstrates analgesic and anticonvulsant effects, helping to reduce pain transmission and prevent excitatory neurotransmitter release that can exacerbate nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high content of valerenic acid, which is known for its sedative and pain-relieving properties.

Seeds: The seeds, although less commonly used than roots and rhizomes, are still utilized in some tinctures due to their lower concentration of valerenic acid and other compounds that may still provide some pain-relieving benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of valeriana officinalis roots and stems in the fall after the plant has finished flowering.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dry place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried valeriana officinalis material into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent extraction.
Combine 1 part of chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to extract active compounds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tinctures helps with nerve pain because they contain flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that interact with the nervous system to produce a sedative effect. This reduces anxiety and stress, common contributors to nerve pain. The tincture's antispasmodic properties also help to calm muscle spasms and inflammation, which can contribute to nerve pain. Additionally, Passiflora incarnata may enhance the release of neurotransmitters that regulate pain perception, providing relief from nerve pain. Its natural anxiolytic properties also promote relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Harmane: Harmane has a sedative and analgesic effect, which can help reduce nerve pain by calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly flavonols and flavanones, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate nerve pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the nervous system.
Isoflavones: Isoflavones, such as isovitexin, have a neuroprotective effect, which can help mitigate nerve pain by reducing excitotoxicity, inflammation, and oxidative stress in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Passiflora incarnata are widely used because they contain the highest concentration of passifloric acid, a key compound responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: The fruits of Passiflora incarnata are commonly used because they contain a rich mix of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that contribute to its nerve pain-relieving properties.

Roots: The roots of Passiflora incarnata are also used because they contain a significant amount of passifloric acid and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 1 pound of dried plant material with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture, also known as St. John's Wort, contains hyperforin and hypericin, which have neuroprotective effects that help to reduce inflammation and pain in the nervous system. It also promotes the regeneration of nerve cells, improving communication between nerve cells and reducing pain transmission. As a result, Hypericum perforatum tinctures have been traditionally used to alleviate nerve pain, numbness, and tingling associated with conditions such as sciatica, neuropathy, and fibromyalgia.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a modulator of neurotransmitter levels, including serotonin and dopamine, which helps to alleviate nerve pain by regulating mood and reducing inflammation.
Naphthodianthrones (mainly Hypericin): Exhibits potent antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that contribute to nerve pain.
Flavonoids (mainly Kaempferol and Quercetin): Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most widely used part due to their high content of hypericin and hyperforin, which contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are used for their high concentration of hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have a positive impact on nerve pain and inflammation.
Stems: They are often used in combination with leaves and flowers to create a comprehensive tincture that can address nerve pain from multiple angles.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 500 milliliters of vodka or 75 percent glycerin for tincture preparation.
Combine the dried flowers with vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Label the tincture and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system. Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic properties, it reduces inflammation and promotes relaxation, alleviating discomfort and tension. The tincture also contains sedative compounds that promote better sleep, which is essential for nerve recovery. By calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation, Avena sativa tinctures can provide relief from nerve pain, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation in the body. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce nerve pain by minimizing inflammation and oxidative stress in the affected areas.
Avenacosides: These saponins have neuroprotective effects, which help alleviate nerve pain by protecting nerve cells from damage and promoting their regeneration.

Furofuran lignans: These phenolic compounds have anti-oxidative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce nerve pain by neutralizing free radicals and minimizing inflammation in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which may help reduce inflammation and relieve nerve pain.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and avenanthramides, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate nerve pain.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may have anti-inflammatory properties and help relieve nerve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh avena sativa roots and leaves by cutting them with scissors to prevent damage.
Dry the harvested avena sativa in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve potency.
Combine 1 part of dried avena sativa with 2 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties and its ability to block pain pathways in the body. The bioactive compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with nerve damage. Additionally, ginger's analgesic and antispasmodic properties help to calm nerve irritation and reduce pain. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from various types of nerve pain, including neuropathy and arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce nerve pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Shogaols: Shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, contributing to nerve pain relief.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to block pain signals and reduce inflammation in the nervous system, providing relief from nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are another commonly used part, as they contain a rich mixture of active compounds, including essential oils and phenolic acids, that contribute to their therapeutic benefits for nerve pain.
Buds: Buds are sometimes used for making tinctures, as they are rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help alleviate nerve pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and 750 milliliters of 80% ethanol.
Chop the rhizomes into small pieces and combine them with the ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid, away from children and pets.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with nerve pain because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture contains compounds such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have a positive effect on nerve function. These compounds can help to reduce inflammation and block pain signals, providing relief from nerve pain. Additionally, the tincture's calming effects may help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate nerve pain, creating a sense of overall well-being and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, which help reduce nerve pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds and promoting the survival of neurons.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a sedative effect, which helps alleviate nerve pain by reducing anxiety, promoting relaxation, and regulating the nervous system.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce nerve pain by neutralizing free radicals, reducing inflammation, and protecting neurons from damage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary source of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nerve pain.
Leaves: They contain a similar composition of essential oils as the flowers, providing similar benefits for nerve pain relief.
Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia also contain essential oils that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a useful part for tincture production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried flowers of lavandula angustifolia in the morning to capture optimal oil content.
Combine 250 grams of the harvested flowers with 1 liter of 50 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with nerve pain because it has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba work synergistically to improve blood flow and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to nerve pain. By enhancing nitric oxide production and relaxing blood vessels, Ginkgo biloba tinctures may help alleviate nerve inflammation and promote healing. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to soothe and calm nerve-related discomfort, providing relief for those suffering from conditions like neuropathy and fibromyalgia.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help alleviate nerve pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected areas.
Bilobalide: It helps alleviate nerve pain by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and reducing excitotoxicity, which can lead to nerve damage.
Ginkgolides: They help alleviate nerve pain by blocking the action of pro-inflammatory mediators and reducing pain transmission in the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba to make tinctures for nerve pain due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Seeds: The seeds contain ginkgolides, which have neuroprotective properties and are often used to treat nerve pain and other neurological conditions.

Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba contain a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are used to make tinctures for nerve pain and other conditions due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of vodka with 25% alcohol content.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every week.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the resulting ginkgo biloba tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cannabis sativa tinctures helps with nerve pain because of their unique combination of cannabinoids and terpenes, which work together to reduce inflammation and relax the nervous system. The cannabinoids, particularly THC and CBD, interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to modulate pain perception and promote analgesia. Additionally, the terpenes present in the tinctures have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which further contribute to their pain-relieving effects, making them a popular natural remedy for managing nerve pain.
remedy_constituents: Cannabinoids: These constituents have a direct impact on the body's endocannabinoid system, which plays a significant role in regulating pain perception, including nerve pain.

Terpenes (specifically, beta-caryophyllene and beta-pinene): These terpenes have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce nerve pain by decreasing inflammation and blocking pain signals.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid and sinapic acid): These phenolic acids exhibit strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help mitigate nerve damage, reduce oxidative stress, and alleviate pain associated with nerve inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in cannabidiol (CBD), a non-psychoactive compound that can help alleviate nerve pain.
Flowers: High in cannabinoids, including THC and CBD, which can provide relief from nerve pain and inflammation.
Buds: Similar to flowers, buds contain a high concentration of cannabinoids, making them effective for nerve pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 200 grams of mature cannabis sativa flowers for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 70°f for 7 to 10 days.
Use a coffee grinder to finely chop 30 grams of dried flowers and 10 grams of root material.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol and 250 milliliters of glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking daily.

remedies.remedy_desc: Corydalis yanhusuo tinctures helps with nerve pain because it contains a unique combination of alkaloids that interact with opioid receptors in the brain, providing a natural analgesic effect. The tincture's active compounds, including dehydrocorybulbine and tetrahydroberberine, have been shown to reduce inflammation and modulate pain signals, leading to a decrease in nerve pain symptoms. Additionally, Corydalis yanhusuo has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various types of pain, including neuropathic pain, making it a promising natural remedy for nerve pain relief.
remedy_constituents: Corydaline: This alkaloid has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its pain-relieving effects on nerve pain.
Berberine: Berberine has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with nerve pain.
Yanhusuine: This alkaloid has analgesic and sedative effects, which can help to reduce nerve pain and promote relaxation and sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain the highest concentration of active compounds responsible for pain relief, including dehydrocorybulbine (DHCB) and tetrahydroberberine.
Stems: The stems also contain tetrahydroberberine, although in smaller amounts compared to the roots and rhizomes, but still contribute to the overall efficacy of the tincture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh or dried corydalis yanhusuo roots in the spring or fall season.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 500 milliliters of 80-100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with nerve pain because they contain compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, alleviating nerve pain and discomfort. Additionally, glycyrrhizin may also block the action of enzymes that contribute to pain transmission, providing relief from nerve pain. By addressing the underlying causes of nerve pain, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures offer a natural and effective solution for managing this condition.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nerves, thereby alleviating nerve pain.
Liquiritin: It exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory activities, which help to block pain signals and reduce inflammation in the nerves, providing relief from nerve pain.

Liquorice flavonoids (specifically isoliquiritigenin): They have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, contributing to the alleviation of nerve pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves have a similar composition to the roots and are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: The stems, although less commonly used than the roots or leaves, also contain glycosides and other compounds that contribute to their pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full maturity.
Clean and dry the roots thoroughly to prevent contamination and ensure optimal extraction.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Mix one part of the chopped roots with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar occasionally.
